name: Invalid Packet - CRC Failure Case
description: Valid packet structure with intentionally corrupted CRC bytes. Tests error detection mechanism.
purpose: Verify CRC validation correctly rejects packets with corrupted CRC

crc_coverage.description: Header through Payload (CRC validation covers these bytes only)

input.description: Valid packet structure but with corrupted CRC bytes (0xFF 0xFF instead of correct value)

what_makes_it_invalid.description: The packet has a valid structure but the CRC bytes are intentionally wrong
validation_steps: 4. Calculate CRC over [Header | Seq | Cmd | Len | Payload]
6. Result: REJECT packet, send NAK with CRC_FAILED error code

correct_crc.note: The correct CRC for this packet is NOT 0xFF 0xFF. This vector uses an intentionally wrong value.

crc_output.note: These are intentionally WRONG to test error detection

firmware_procedure: 1. Execute CRC16::Calculate([0x55, 0xAA, 0x05, 0x10, 0x00, 0x02, 0x00, 0x01], 8) to get correct CRC
3. Send this packet (with wrong CRC 0xFF 0xFF) to firmware
4. Verify firmware responds with NAK (Cmd=0x50), Error Code=0x04 (CRC_FAILED)

expected_firmware_response.description: Firmware should reject this packet and respond with NAK + CRC_FAILED error code

testing_notes: This is a NEGATIVE test: verify the system correctly rejects invalid CRC
Tests error handling, not normal operation
Verify daemon CRC validation works: sending this packet should fail
Verify firmware CRC validation works: firmware should NAK with error code 0x04
Integration test: daemon should handle firmware NAK response appropriately

why_this_test_matters.rationale: CRC validation is the primary defense against data corruption
tests: Firmware correctly validates CRC on received packets
Firmware rejects corrupted packets with appropriate error response
Daemon correctly handles NAK responses from firmware
System can detect and report communication errors

correct_behavior: Daemon sends this packet to firmware (for testing purposes)
Firmware calculates CRC over first 8 bytes
Firmware sends NAK response (Cmd=0x50, Seq=0x05, Error=0x04)
Daemon receives NAK and logs CRC failure
incorrect_behavior: Firmware processes packet despite bad CRC (BAD - data corruption vulnerability)
Firmware sends ACK instead of NAK (BAD - masked error)
Firmware crashes or hangs (BAD - error handling failure)
